Title: The biosynthesis of thymol, carvacrol, and thymohydroquinone in Lamiaceae proceeds via cytochrome P450s and a short-chain dehydrogenase
Journal: Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences of the United States of America

Pathway_Genes.Source_Species_Variety: T type; C type; G type chemotypes
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: T type; C type; G type chemotypes
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Upstream entry step to thymol/carvacrol branch
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Flavor and aroma compounds; antibacterial and anti-spasmodic properties; upstream precursors to bioactive thymohydroquinone/thymoquinone
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Leaves and other aerial tissues of Lamiaceae, commonly glandular trichomes
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms immediate precursor for target products
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key entry-step enzyme; not explicitly established as rate-limiting in this paper
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Transcript levels positively correlated with high thymol/carvacrol in T and C chemotypes and very low in G chemotype leaves
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Leaves of thymol- and carvacrol-rich chemotypes
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Provides γ-terpinene precursor pool enabling downstream thymol/carvacrol biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Gene expression correlation across chemotypes; heterologous bacterial expression; in vitro enzyme assay with GDP; transient pathway reconstitution in Nicotiana benthamiana using OvTPS2 analog for full pathway
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TvTPS2 transcript abundance correlates with phenolic monoterpene-rich chemotypes of T. vulgaris. 2. Recombinant TvTPS2 converts GDP to γ-terpinene. 3. γ-Terpinene generated by TPS2 serves as the direct precursor for downstream CYP71D/SDR-mediated thymol and carvacrol formation.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful upstream enzyme for reconstruction and engineering of thymol/carvacrol pathway when stacked with CYP71D and SDR genes
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Low product levels in N. benthamiana may reflect limited GDP availability or lack of glandular trichomes
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: OvTPS2 from oregano was used for complete in planta reconstitution in N. benthamiana and was sufficient with TvCYP71D179-T and TvSDR1 to produce thymol

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Thymus vulgaris; Nicotiana benthamiana; Saccharomyces cerevisiae
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: T chemotype; NA; WAT11 yeast expression strain
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxidizes γ-terpinene at an allylic position, inferred mainly C-3 hydroxylation, to unstable cyclohexadienol intermediate en route to thymol; without SDR, the intermediate rearranges to p-cymene
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Unstable C-3 cyclohexadienol intermediate; p-cymene artifact in absence of SDR
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Aroma/flavor compound with antibacterial and anti-spasmodic properties; precursor to thymohydroquinone/thymoquinone
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Leaves and aerial tissues; glandular trichomes inferred
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms immediate precursor of target product
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Branch-switch/key control step for thymol versus carvacrol outcome
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highest in T type leaves, lowest in C type, intermediate in G type
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transient expression in N. benthamiana enables thymol production when coexpressed with TvSDR1 and γ-terpinene supplied
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes thymol formation from γ-terpinene, with minor carvacrol in some assays
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Chemotype expression analysis; yeast microsome assay; limonene product profiling; in vitro coupled assay with TvSDR1; transient expression in N. benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TvCYP71D179-T expression correlates with thymol-rich thyme chemotype. 2. In yeast with γ-terpinene alone it yields p-cymene, indicating unstable oxidized intermediate formation. 3. Combined with TvSDR1 it produces thymol as the major product in vitro and in N. benthamiana, defining the thymol branch.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: γ-terpinene feeding in N. benthamiana leaves
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Endoplasmic reticulum membrane/cytosolic surface inferred for CYPs
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: A strong candidate for metabolic engineering of thymol-rich plants or heterologous production systems
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: In absence of SDR flux is lost to p-cymene; regiospecificity may show minor product promiscuity
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Also hydroxylates (+)-limonene mainly to trans-isopiperitenol, supporting allylic hydroxylation chemistry

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Thymus vulgaris; Nicotiana benthamiana; Saccharomyces cerevisiae
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: C chemotype; NA; WAT11 yeast expression strain
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxidizes γ-terpinene at an allylic position, inferred mainly C-6 hydroxylation, to unstable cyclohexadienol intermediate en route to carvacrol; without SDR, the intermediate rearranges to p-cymene
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Unstable C-6 cyclohexadienol intermediate; p-cymene artifact in absence of SDR
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Aroma/flavor compound with antibacterial and anti-spasmodic properties; precursor to thymohydroquinone/thymoquinone
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Leaves and aerial tissues; glandular trichomes inferred
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms immediate precursor of target product
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Branch-switch/key control step for thymol versus carvacrol outcome
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Almost exclusively expressed in C type leaves; only trace transcripts in G and T types
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transient expression in N. benthamiana enables carvacrol production when coexpressed with TvSDR1 and γ-terpinene supplied
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Chemotype expression analysis; yeast microsome assay; limonene product profiling; in vitro coupled assay with TvSDR1; transient expression in N. benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TvCYP71D180-C expression strongly associates with carvacrol-rich thyme chemotype. 2. Its isolated CYP assay yields p-cymene from γ-terpinene, consistent with unstable hydroxylated intermediate formation. 3. Combined with TvSDR1 it produces carvacrol as the major product in vitro and in planta, defining the carvacrol branch.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: γ-terpinene feeding in N. benthamiana leaves
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Endoplasmic reticulum membrane/cytosolic surface inferred for CYPs
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for engineering carvacrol-dominant chemotypes or heterologous production
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: In absence of SDR flux is lost to p-cymene
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: With (+)-limonene, major product is cis-carveol, consistent with C-6 hydroxylase-like regioselectivity

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Thymus vulgaris; Origanum vulgare; Nicotiana benthamiana; Saccharomyces cerevisiae
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: T/C/G chemotypes; accession USA; NA; WAT11 yeast expression strain
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxidizes γ-terpinene to unstable cyclohexadienol intermediate(s) that can be converted with TvSDR1 to thymol and/or carvacrol depending on expression system
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Unstable cyclohexadienol intermediate(s); p-cymene artifact in absence of SDR
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Leaves and aerial tissues; glandular trichomes inferred
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms immediate precursor(s) to target products
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Not established; branch contribution enzyme with context-dependent regioselectivity
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Similar transcript levels in all three T. vulgaris chemotypes
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Oregano homolog OvCYP71D507 correlated with thymol levels in accession USA tissues
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Produces mainly thymol in yeast coupled assay but both carvacrol and low thymol in N. benthamiana with TvSDR1
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Expression analysis; yeast microsome assay; limonene product profiling; in vitro coupled assay with TvSDR1; transient expression in N. benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TvCYP71D507 is a functional γ-terpinene-oxidizing CYP71D. 2. In combination with TvSDR1 it yields thymol in yeast assays but both carvacrol and low thymol in N. benthamiana, showing expression-system-dependent regioselectivity. 3. The oregano homolog correlates with thymol accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: γ-terpinene feeding in N. benthamiana leaves
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Endoplasmic reticulum membrane/cytosolic surface inferred for CYPs
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential flexible catalyst for mixed phenolic monoterpene production, though product specificity may be context dependent
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Product ratio varies between yeast and plant systems
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Contains isoleucine substitution at position analogous to Mentha F363, suggested as possible contributor to variable regioselectivity

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: accession USA and other accessions; WAT11 yeast expression strain
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxidizes γ-terpinene to unstable intermediate that, with TvSDR1, yields thymol
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Unstable cyclohexadienol intermediate en route to thymol
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Leaves, inflorescences, stems, young leaves; glandular trichomes inferred
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms immediate precursor of target product
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Branch-associated enzyme; not established as rate limiting
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports thymol formation in coupled assays
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transcript discovery; correlation with metabolite profile; in vitro coupled assay with TvSDR1
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OvCYP71D178 was identified from oregano as a CYP71D candidate in phenolic monoterpene metabolism. 2. In coupled assays with TvSDR1 and γ-terpinene it forms thymol. 3. Its expression correlates with thymol-associated oregano tissues/accessions.
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Endoplasmic reticulum membrane/cytosolic surface inferred for CYPs
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate enzyme for thymol pathway engineering in oregano-related systems
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Unstable intermediate may be diverted to p-cymene without SDR

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: accession USA and other accessions; WAT11 yeast expression strain
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxidizes γ-terpinene to unstable intermediate that, with TvSDR1, yields carvacrol
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Unstable cyclohexadienol intermediate en route to carvacrol
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Leaves, inflorescences, stems, young leaves; glandular trichomes inferred
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms immediate precursor of target product
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Branch-associated enzyme; not established as rate limiting
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports carvacrol formation in coupled assays
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transcript discovery; metabolite-expression correlation; in vitro coupled assay with TvSDR1
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OvCYP71D181 is associated with carvacrol-rich oregano tissues. 2. In coupled assays with TvSDR1 and γ-terpinene it forms carvacrol. 3. It supports the existence of a carvacrol-specific CYP71D branch in oregano.
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Endoplasmic reticulum membrane/cytosolic surface inferred for CYPs
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Unstable intermediate may be diverted to p-cymene without SDR

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxidizes γ-terpinene to unstable intermediate that, with TvSDR1, yields carvacrol
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Unstable cyclohexadienol intermediate en route to carvacrol
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms immediate precursor of target product
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Branch-associated enzyme; not established as rate limiting
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports carvacrol formation in coupled assay
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: In vitro coupled assay with TvSDR1
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TvCYP71D182 is one of the thyme CYP71Ds capable of converting γ-terpinene toward carvacrol when paired with TvSDR1. 2. It reinforces that multiple CYP71D paralogs contribute to the carvacrol branch.
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Endoplasmic reticulum membrane/cytosolic surface inferred for CYPs
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Additional carvacrol-branch candidate for pathway engineering
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Likely requires SDR coexpression to prevent p-cymene loss

Pathway_Genes.Source_Species_Variety: C type; G type; T type
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species: Thyme; Oregano; Nicotiana benthamiana; Escherichia coli recombinant protein; yeast coupled assay context
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Thymus vulgaris; Origanum vulgare; Nicotiana benthamiana; Escherichia coli; Saccharomyces cerevisiae
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Shared oxidation step downstream of CYP71D branch point
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxidizes unstable allylic monoterpene alcohol/cyclohexadienol intermediates generated from γ-terpinene to the corresponding ketones, which then aromatize to thymol or carvacrol
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: CYP71D-derived cyclohexadienol intermediates; also trans-/cis-isopiperitenol; trans-/cis-carveol
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Corresponding allylic ketones leading to thymol or carvacrol; isopiperitenone; carvone in test assays
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Leaves and aerial tissues; glandular trichomes inferred
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Midstream oxidation immediately after CYP hydroxylation
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms immediate precursor that aromatizes to target product
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Essential shared step; not explicitly called rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: No major differences among C, T, and G thyme chemotype leaves
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Oregano homolog OvSDR1 correlated with thymol levels in accession USA tissues
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required with CYP71Ds for efficient thymol/carvacrol formation and reduction of flux loss to p-cymene
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq identification; recombinant protein assay; substrate specificity assays; in vitro coupled assays with CYP71Ds; transient coexpression in N. benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TvSDR1 is an SDR110C enzyme homologous to mint isopiperitenol dehydrogenase. 2. It oxidizes allylic monoterpene alcohols to ketones and does not accept p-cymene. 3. Together with CYP71D enzymes it is necessary for thymol and carvacrol biosynthesis from γ-terpinene, preventing loss of unstable intermediates to p-cymene.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: γ-terpinene feeding in N. benthamiana leaves for pathway tests
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Cytosol inferred; no transit peptide predicted
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Essential partner gene to stack with γ-terpinene synthase and CYP71Ds for metabolic engineering of aromatic monoterpenes
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Without close coupling to CYP step, unstable intermediates may rearrange to p-cymene
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Contains motifs typical of classical SDRs and likely prefers NAD(H) based on Asp52

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Origanum vulgare; Nicotiana benthamiana; Saccharomyces cerevisiae
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Downstream hydroxylation of thymol/carvacrol to thymohydroquinone
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates thymol or carvacrol at the aromatic position opposite the existing hydroxyl group to form thymohydroquinone
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Mature leaves highest, then inflorescences, stems, young leaves in oregano accession USA
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key downstream biosynthetic enzyme; not established as rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed in thymohydroquinone-producing oregano tissues; consistent with transcriptomic and RT-qPCR data
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transient expression in N. benthamiana leaf disks enables thymohydroquinone formation after substrate feeding
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Converts both thymol and carvacrol to thymohydroquinone
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: PCC-based candidate discovery; transient expression in N. benthamiana with substrate feeding; yeast expression and feeding assays; RT-qPCR tissue validation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OvCYP76S40 was identified through tissue metabolite-transcript correlation as a thymohydroquinone-pathway candidate. 2. Heterologous expression in N. benthamiana and yeast showed conversion of both thymol and carvacrol to thymohydroquinone. 3. Minor thymoquinone also appeared in yeast, likely partly from spontaneous oxidation of thymohydroquinone.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Feeding of thymol or carvacrol to transformed N. benthamiana leaf disks and yeast cultures
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Endoplasmic reticulum membrane/cytosolic surface inferred for CYPs
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful downstream enzyme for engineering thymohydroquinone production from thymol/carvacrol platforms
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: May generate some thymoquinone indirectly or as minor side product; endogenous background hydroxylation exists in N. benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Accepts both thymol and carvacrol, indicating limited substrate specificity between the two aromatic monoterpene isomers

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Origanum vulgare; Nicotiana benthamiana; Saccharomyces cerevisiae
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Downstream hydroxylation of thymol/carvacrol to thymohydroquinone
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates thymol or carvacrol at the aromatic position opposite the existing hydroxyl group to form thymohydroquinone
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Mature leaves highest, then inflorescences, stems, young leaves in oregano accession USA
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key downstream biosynthetic enzyme; not established as rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed in thymohydroquinone-producing oregano tissues; confirmed by RT-qPCR
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transient expression in N. benthamiana leaf disks enables thymohydroquinone formation after substrate feeding
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Converts both thymol and carvacrol to thymohydroquinone
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: PCC-based candidate discovery; transient expression in N. benthamiana with substrate feeding; yeast expression and feeding assays; RT-qPCR tissue validation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OvCYP736A300 was identified by oregano tissue omics and metabolite correlation analysis. 2. It catalyzes conversion of both thymol and carvacrol to thymohydroquinone in N. benthamiana and yeast. 3. It represents a structurally distinct CYP family converging on the same biochemical step as OvCYP76S40.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Feeding of thymol or carvacrol to transformed N. benthamiana leaf disks and yeast cultures
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Endoplasmic reticulum membrane/cytosolic surface inferred for CYPs
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Alternative downstream hydroxylase for engineering thymohydroquinone production
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: May yield minor thymoquinone through enzyme or spontaneous oxidation route
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Functionally convergent with OvCYP76S40 despite belonging to a different CYP subfamily
